positiveClaims: The existing release claim mode decision gate is product-bound as a bounded Review Bridge mode decision boundary.
Mode decision acceptance requires accepted 12B release claim dossier provenance and accepted 12C release claim admission provenance.
The binding derives claimId, dossierId, and matrixId from accepted upstream provenance rather than trusting caller-supplied decision ids.
PR_MODE remains internal proof only and does not create user-facing release authority.

RELEASE_MODE is accepted only when releaseEvidenceId, releaseEvidenceHash, inputHash, outputHash, commandRunDigest, claimId, dossierId, and matrixId are present and match accepted upstream provenance.
Caller-supplied claimId, dossierId, and matrixId mismatches block the mode decision gate.
The immediate downstream 12E attestation and 12F packet emit gates re-evaluate accepted upstream payloads to block forged accepted 12D or 12E envelopes.

The binding is mode decision truth only; it is not attestation completion, packet emit completion, release execution, release publication, user-facing release, import, export, apply, storage truth, or release readiness truth.
nonClaims: No release readiness is claimed.
No user-facing release is claimed.
No release attestation completion is claimed.
No packet emit completion is claimed.
No release execution completion is claimed.
No release publication completion is claimed.
No Word support is claimed.
No Google Docs support is claimed.
No DOCX support expansion is claimed.
No import support is claimed.
No export support is claimed.
No review import completion is claimed.
No roundtrip is claimed.
No layout parity is claimed.
No full fidelity is claimed.
No automatic apply is claimed.
No structural apply is claimed.
No comment auto-apply is claimed.
No manuscript write is performed by release claim mode decision binding.
No project truth write is performed by release claim mode decision binding.
No receipt or recovery evidence is created by release claim mode decision binding.
No DOCX import safe-create behavior is changed.
No DOCX export behavior is changed.
No import/export MVP closeout is widened.
No PDF, EPUB, HTML, Mindmap, cloud sync, cross-platform readiness, packaged readiness, or Y9 admission is claimed.
layerDecisions: Mode decision binding is release-claim mode decision truth only and accepts only a bounded internal release-claim candidate.
Mode decision gate acceptance does not become project truth by itself.
Mode decision gate acceptance does not create a release packet by itself.
Mode decision gate acceptance does not admit execution or publication.
Mode decision gate acceptance does not prove full fidelity, layout parity, roundtrip, import correctness, or export correctness.
This contour is not an attestation completion contour.
This contour is not a packet emit completion contour.
This contour is not a user-facing boundary contour.
This contour is not a release execution contour.
This contour is not a release publication contour.
Mode decision binding is not a parser and does not inspect external documents.
Mode decision binding is not content import and does not create scenes.
Mode decision binding is not Review import automation and does not activate a Review session.
Mode decision binding is not an apply plan and does not authorize mutation.
Mode decision binding is not safe write and does not create receipt or recovery evidence.
Mode decision binding is not export and does not alter DOCX output.
Review Bridge may reference accepted mode decision output as bounded internal chain evidence, but accepted mode decision output does not become project truth.

machineChecks.command: node --test release claim mode decision, attestation, and packet emit contracts
node --test release claim mode decision binding targeted contracts
node --test release claim decision through execution chain contracts

agentAudits.finding: Confirmed the next contour is canonical as a bounded product-binding over the existing CONTOUR_12D_RELEASE_CLAIM_MODE_DECISION_GATE after delivered 12C admission binding, with mandatory nonclaims against release readiness, user-facing release, execution, publication, import/export, apply, receipt, recovery, project truth, storage truth, safe-create change, export change, MVP widening, and Y9.

Identified a false-green risk where caller-supplied mode decision ids and forged accepted 12D or 12E envelopes could poison downstream binding; resolved by deriving 12D ids from accepted upstream provenance and by re-evaluating 12D/12E provenance in 12E/12F guards.

nextStep: finish delivery chain, then continue with the next owner-selected Review Bridge boundary
